text: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the biological, psychological and social sciences underpinning nursing practice
Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of human development, health and illness across the lifespan
Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of evidence-based practice, research methodology and quality improvement in nursing
Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of ethical, legal and governance frameworks governing nursing practice
Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of pharmacology and safe medicines management
Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of health policy, legislation and its impact on nursing and care delivery
Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of public health, health promotion, disease prevention and the wider determinants of health

Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of professional nursing identity, the regulatory framework (including The Code), professional role development and the values underpinning the nursing profession
Critically analyse and evaluate evidence from multiple sources to inform nursing practice
Apply evidence-based reasoning to clinical decision-making, care planning and nursing interventions
Synthesise knowledge across disciplines to inform holistic, person-centred nursing practice
Critically reflect on professional practice and use reflection to identify areas for development
Formulate and address research, audit and quality improvement questions relevant to nursing
Evaluate the safety, quality and effectiveness of nursing care and apply findings to improve practice
Analyse health policy and organisational context and their implications for nursing practice
Demonstrate competent clinical nursing skills across a range of settings and patient groups
Undertake holistic, person-centred nursing assessment and develop evidence-based, individualised care plans
Communicate effectively with patients, families, carers and colleagues across all settings and contexts
Safely manage and administer medicines in accordance with NMC standards, local policy and pharmacological principles
Lead, coordinate and delegate nursing care, working effectively within and across interdisciplinary teams
Use digital technologies and information management systems to support safe, effective care delivery
Manage own learning and professional development, maintaining a reflective portfolio and personal development plan
Work effectively and collaboratively as part of multi-disciplinary and inter-professional teams
Demonstrate professional values, ethical conduct, personal integrity, courage and the duty of candour
Advocate effectively for patients, carers and communities, promoting equity of access and person-centred care
Adapt practice to diverse cultural, social and individual needs, demonstrating cultural competence and inclusion
Apply leadership and management principles, demonstrating resilience, emotional intelligence and political awareness
Engage proactively in quality improvement, patient safety initiatives and risk management
Demonstrate commitment to lifelong learning and continuous professional development as a registered nurse
